articles.title: Canada lose home advantage for last 32 after Switzerland win to take top spot
articles.description: Switzerland topped Group B with a 2-1 win over Canada with both teams going through to the knockout stage

articles.title: Trump accused of showing ‘complete indifference’ to Americans’ living costs after cancelling housing bill signing – US politics live
articles.description: President was due to sign deal aimed at making life more affordable for Americans but shelved event to pressure Congress to pass restrictive voting bill

articles.title: Europe heatwave live: UK breaks temperature record for June as parts of France hit 40C – as it happened
articles.description: West Sussex reports temperature of 35.8C, beating previous record from 1976; red weather alert extended to 72 of France’s 96 mainland departments

more.title: Ads in our chats? WhatsApp Plus rollout in SA raises questions
more.description: The optional service does not affect standard messaging and is being described as a “soft launch” of premium perks.

more.title: Huawei ICT Academy Instructor inspires South Africa’s youth to keep learning for the future
more.description: Neo Rameetse's Huawei ICT Academy journey highlights how practical training, continuous learning and industry certification can help young people build careers in ICT.

more.title: Buses meant to transport Malawians for repatriation delayed
more.description: The latest development comes as thousands of foreign nationals continue leaving South Africa ahead of an informal 30 June deadline set by anti-illegal immigration groups.

more.title: The most valuable currency in South Africa’s ride-hailing economy? Trust
more.description: Difficult to earn and remarkably easy to lose, trust has become one of the most important drivers of loyalty in South Africa’s increasingly competitive ride-hailing market. For commuters, trust amounts to peace of mind, knowing what they are paying and feeling confident that the service they use is accountable and reliable. Drivers need to be [...]

more.title: Smart ID rollout hits 200 bank branches
more.description: Home Affairs expands smart ID replacement, processing more than 250 000 transactions in just over three months.

more.title: Nedbank: Growth slows, inflation peaks and rates stay higher for longer
more.description: Bank expects price pressures to ease later this year, but sees no immediate scope for interest rate cuts.

more.title: Musa Mseleku on being the undisputed face of polygamy
more.description: Host of "Mnakwethu", Musa Mseleku, explores polygamy's evolving role in South Africa

more.title: Mpumalanga parties fine-tune messaging to reach grassroots voters
more.description: Political parties in Mpumalanga are increasingly tailoring their campaigns around the issues dominating community conversations as the race towards the November 4 local government elections begins to intensify. From free driver’s licences and sports tournaments to anti-illegal immigration campaigns and border security rhetoric, parties appear to be betting that voters will respond more readily to [...]

more.title: South Africa raises lemon export forecast as favourable conditions boost crop prospects
more.description: The Citrus Growers’ Association of Southern Africa has revised its lemon export estimates for the 2026 season upwards, citing favourable growing conditions and effective logistical management, despite challenges posed by recent floods.

more.title: Relebohile Mofokeng’s mother urges him to pack his bags and leave Orlando Pirates
more.description: Orlando Pirates may be running out of time to keep Relebohile Mofokeng, with his mother adamant that Europe should be next.

more.title: PSL club announce former Kaizer Chiefs co-coach
more.description: Former Kaizer Chiefs co-coach Khalil Ben Youssef has officially been appointed as the new head coach of Durban City ahead of the 2026/27 season.

more.title: Doctor Khumalo sends clear message as Bafana face crucial Fifa World Cup clash
more.description: Bafana Bafana face a crucial Fifa World Cup match against South Korea, where a draw or victory is essential to advance beyond the group stage, as emphasised by Afcon-winning legend Doctor Khumalo.

more.title: South Africans face higher electricity bills as municipal tariff hikes begin
more.description: As winter temperatures drop across South Africa, households are preparing for another increase in monthly expenses as electricity prices rise from July. From 1 July, municipalities across the country will introduce their annual electricity tariff adjustments, placing additional pressure on families already dealing with higher living costs. The latest increases follow Eskom’s own tariff hike for direct customers, which came... more.title: International platforms can advance cooperation for climate action
more.description: Explore how international cooperation at Climate Action Week is paving the way for effective climate action and what South Africa's role is in this global dialogue.

more.title: SOWETAN | When medicinal plants disappear, SA loses more than biodiversity
more.description: South Africa’s conservation agenda has often treated medicinal plants with less urgency than more visible conservation priorities.
